oracle_data.golden_api_signature: main(stdin: JSON object with feature-specific fields) -> stdout exactly matching public_test_cases expected_output

hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The total spherical length is exactly 0.0 because a single point has no extent to measure.
The total spherical length is exactly 0.0 because an empty or null point array has no coordinates to traverse.
The exact minimum distance between the North Pole (lat 90) and South Pole (lat -90) is approximately 20,906,181.0 meters.

This generated package must cover the full detailed-PRD behavior represented by all authoritative public test case files, not just a subset mentioned in the fuzzy PRD. Public feature files: feature10_path_length, feature11_spherical_polygon_area, feature12_signed_area_orientation, feature13_polygon_containment, feature14_polygon_edge, feature15_path_location, feature16_map_line_containment, feature17_map_line_length, feature18_map_polygon_containment, feature19_map_polygon_edge, feature1_polyline_encoding, feature20_polygon_style_options, feature21_point_components, feature22_weighted_heat_point, feature2_polyline_decoding, feature3_polygon_closedness, feature4_path_simplification, feature5_spherical_heading, feature6_spherical_offset, feature7_spherical_offset_origin, feature8_spherical_interpolation, feature9_spherical_distance. Feature descriptions include: Compute total spherical length for an ordered coordinate path.; Compute the unsigned area enclosed by a spherical polygon.; Report opposite signed areas when a spherical polygon traversal order is reversed.; Classify whether a query coordinate lies inside a geodesic polygon.; Classify whether a query coordinate lies on or near a polygon edge.; Classify whether a query coordinate lies on or near a geodesic path.; Classify whether a query coordinate lies on a map line object.; Read the spherical length represented by a map line object.; Classify whether a query coordinate is contained by a map polygon object.; Classify whether a query coordinate lies on or near a map polygon object edge.; Encode a coordinate sequence into the compact polyline wire format.; Build polygon drawing options that preserve style values and vertices.; Expose the two numeric components of a planar point in order.; Convert a geographic coordinate into a weighted heat-map point.; Decode a compact polyline string into an ordered coordinate sequence.; Classify a coordinate sequence as closed only when the first and last coordinates are equal.; Simplify a coordinate path while preserving its endpoints.; Compute the initial bearing between two coordinates on a sphere.

The execution adapter must accept exactly the input shapes used by public_test_cases and emit stdout exactly equal to each expected_output. The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, numeric formatting, and deterministic serialization. If prose, fuzzy_prd, context pointers, or older hidden constraints are vague or conflict with expected_output, the public_test_cases expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

context_pointers.vague_reference: requires negative sign convention matching polygon area calc
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The signed area must use the same positive/negative convention as the function handling unsigned enclosed areas (C011), where consistent traversal orientation yields positive values and reversed traversal yields negative values.

context_pointers.vague_reference: intensity structure format follows heat map spec
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The returned structure must match the field names and types defined in the core 'heatmap_point' data structure used throughout the visualization module.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_path_length.json.description: Compute total spherical length for an ordered coordinate path.

feature11_spherical_polygon_area.json.description: Compute the unsigned area enclosed by a spherical polygon.

feature12_signed_area_orientation.json.description: Report opposite signed areas when a spherical polygon traversal order is reversed.

feature13_polygon_containment.json.description: Classify whether a query coordinate lies inside a geodesic polygon.

feature14_polygon_edge.json.description: Classify whether a query coordinate lies on or near a polygon edge.

feature15_path_location.json.description: Classify whether a query coordinate lies on or near a geodesic path.

feature16_map_line_containment.json.description: Classify whether a query coordinate lies on a map line object.

feature17_map_line_length.json.description: Read the spherical length represented by a map line object.

feature18_map_polygon_containment.json.description: Classify whether a query coordinate is contained by a map polygon object.

feature19_map_polygon_edge.json.description: Classify whether a query coordinate lies on or near a map polygon object edge.

feature1_polyline_encoding.json.description: Encode a coordinate sequence into the compact polyline wire format.

feature20_polygon_style_options.json.description: Build polygon drawing options that preserve style values and vertices.

feature21_point_components.json.description: Expose the two numeric components of a planar point in order.

feature22_weighted_heat_point.json.description: Convert a geographic coordinate into a weighted heat-map point.

feature2_polyline_decoding.json.description: Decode a compact polyline string into an ordered coordinate sequence.

feature3_polygon_closedness.json.description: Classify a coordinate sequence as closed only when the first and last coordinates are equal.

feature4_path_simplification.json.description: Simplify a coordinate path while preserving its endpoints.

feature5_spherical_heading.json.description: Compute the initial bearing between two coordinates on a sphere.

feature6_spherical_offset.json.description: Move a coordinate by a spherical distance and heading.

feature7_spherical_offset_origin.json.description: Recover a possible origin coordinate from a destination, distance, and heading.

feature8_spherical_interpolation.json.description: Interpolate along the spherical path between two coordinates.

feature9_spherical_distance.json.description: Compute spherical distance between two coordinates in meters.

_constraints.input_condition: Input points array exists
_constraints.output_rule: Return encoded polyline string followed by a single newline character if points are valid

_constraints.input_condition: Input points array is empty
_constraints.output_rule: Return empty string followed by newline

_constraints.input_condition: Input encoded_polyline string exists
_constraints.output_rule: Return JSON object with count of decoded coordinates and array of coordinates formatted to 6 decimal places

_constraints.input_condition: Input encoded_polyline string is empty or null
_constraints.output_rule: Return zero count and empty coordinates array

_constraints.input_condition: Input points array has >= 2 points AND first point equals last point
_constraints.output_rule: Return classification 'closed'

_constraints.input_condition: Input points array has >= 2 points AND first point does not equal last point
_constraints.output_rule: Return classification 'open'

_constraints.input_condition: Input encoded_path and tolerance_meters provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return simplified coordinate count, first coordinate, and last coordinate after simplification

_constraints.input_condition: Input start and end coordinates provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return initial heading in degrees clockwise from north, normalized to signed degree range

_constraints.input_condition: Input start is North Pole (lat 90, lng doesn't matter) and end is South Pole (lat -90)
_constraints.output_rule: Return 0 degrees (arbitrary direction from pole)

_constraints.input_condition: Input origin, distance_meters, and heading_degrees provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return destination coordinate reached by traveling distance in direction of heading

_constraints.input_condition: Input destination, distance_meters, and heading_degrees are provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return whether an origin is recoverable; if recoverable, return the origin coordinate; otherwise indicate unavailability

_constraints.input_condition: Input start, end, and fraction provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return the coordinate located at the exact spherical interpolation point for the fraction

_constraints.input_condition: Input start and end coordinates provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return spherical distance in meters

_constraints.input_condition: Input points array is empty
_constraints.output_rule: Return total spherical length as 0.0

_constraints.input_condition: Input points array contains exactly 1 point
_constraints.output_rule: Return total spherical length as 0.0

_constraints.input_condition: Input points form a valid closed spherical polygon
_constraints.output_rule: Return unsigned enclosed area in square meters

_constraints.input_condition: Input provided as closed spherical polygon
_constraints.output_rule: Return signed area for original traversal and signed area for reversed traversal (values must be opposite signs)

_constraints.input_condition: Input polygon, query, and geodesic flag provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return classification 'inside' or 'outside' and echo the query coordinate

_constraints.input_condition: Input polygon, query, and geodesic flag provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return classification 'on_edge' or 'not_on_edge'

_constraints.input_condition: Input path and query with geodesic flag provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return classification 'on_path' or 'not_on_path'

_constraints.input_condition: Input line and query with geodesic flag provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return classification 'on_line' or 'not_on_line'

_constraints.input_condition: Input line and query where query is extremely close to the line (< tolerance threshold)
_constraints.output_rule: Parallel to exact point-on-segment detection, returning 'on_line'

_constraints.input_condition: Input empty line array and geodesic flag provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return spherical length as 0.0

_constraints.input_condition: Input polygon and query provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return whether query is strictly contained or not

_constraints.input_condition: Query point coincides exactly with a vertex of the polygon
_constraints.output_rule: Treat vertex as strictly inside

_constraints.input_condition: Input polygon and query provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return whether query lies within a specific edge tolerance distance of the polygon edges

_constraints.input_condition: Query point has minute deviation from vertex (e.g., +0.0000005)
_constraints.output_rule: Determine coverage based on edge normal distance vs. tolerance; if within tolerance, classify as 'on_edge'

_constraints.input_condition: Input stroke_width, stroke_color, and points provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return a mapping object preserving style values and vertex coordinates

_constraints.input_condition: Input x and y provided
_constraints.output_rule: Return array with numeric components [x, y] in order

_constraints.input_condition: Input coordinate provided (and optionally intensity)
_constraints.output_rule: Return a structure representing a weighted heat-map point with coordinate and intensity fields

_constraints.input_condition: Only coordinate provided, no intensity
_constraints.output_rule: Default intensity to 1.0

_constraints.input_condition: Both coordinate and intensity provided
_constraints.output_rule: Use provided intensity values exactly

_constraints.input_condition: Input demonstrates poles (90/-90 latitude, same longitude)
_constraints.output_rule: Compute exact minimum distance between poles (approx 20,906,181 meters)

_constraints.input_condition: Any operation involving coordinate conversion or calculation
_constraints.output_rule: Result lat/lng values must be rounded or truncated to 6 decimal places

_constraints.input_condition: Input validation (e.g., missing required fields, out of range lat/lng)
_constraints.output_rule: Throw specific exception type or return Err result; do not crash with generic runtime error

_constraints.input_condition: Any operation
_constraints.output_rule: Ensure return types are strictly defined (e.g., Result<T, E> or Option<T>) avoiding nulls in critical paths

hide.oracle_response: The total spherical length is exactly 0.0 because a single point has no extent to measure.
The total spherical length is exactly 0.0 because an empty or null point array has no coordinates to traverse.
The exact minimum distance between the North Pole (lat 90) and South Pole (lat -90) is approximately 20,906,181.0 meters.
